product_overview: A lightweight, browser-based programmable workout timer that supports Stopwatch, Preset workout templates (Tabata, EMOM, AMRAP, etc.), and a Custom Builder for user-defined sequences. The UI is mobile-first, embeddable (Notion-compatible), and runs entirely in the browser (React + Vite + TypeScript).
core_goals: Provide three core modes: Stopwatch, Preset Timer, and Custom Timer Builder to cover casual and advanced interval training (see PRD pages 1-3).
Offer clear runtime visuals: Circular countdown, Workout Timeline, and Timer Display while keeping the app lightweight and embeddable.
Enable users to quickly start, pause/resume, skip, and reset workouts with responsive mobile-first controls.
Ship an MVP runtime engine that compiles blocks into a step queue and executes them in-browser without server-side components.

features.name: Stopwatch Mode
features.description: A simple stopwatch that counts up from zero with start, pause/resume, and reset controls accessible from the main app page.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Stopwatch" tab -> Click "Start" button to begin timing -> Observe timer counting up -> Click "Pause" button to pause timing -> Observe timer frozen -> Click "Start" button to resume timing -> Observe timer resume counting -> Click "Stop" button to reset timing -> Observe timer reset to 00:00:00

Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Stopwatch" tab -> Click "Pause" button before starting -> Observe no change or disabled control indicating timer is not running -> Click "Stop" button -> Observe timer remains at 00:00:00

features.name: Preset Timer Mode
features.description: Select a pre-configured workout template (Tabata, EMOM, AMRAP, etc.), compile it into a runtime queue, and execute it with timeline and circular countdown visuals.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Presets" tab -> Scroll through carousel of predefined workout templates -> Click a template card to select it -> Click "Start [Template Name]" to begin the compiled workout queue -> Observe Circular Timer countdown and Workout Timeline -> Click "Start/Pause" pill button to pause playback -> Observe playback paused -> Click "Start/Pause" to resume playback -> Click "Skip" arrow to move to the next step early -> Observe timeline advances to the next step
Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Presets" tab -> Click empty area of carousel (no template selected) -> Click the Start control -> Observe no action or a prompt to select a template

Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Presets" tab -> Click a template card to select it -> Click "Start [Template Name]" to begin the compiled workout queue -> Observe visual countdown -> Expect audio cues -> Audio does not play due to browser autoplay policy until user interacts with the document (see known limitations in codebase)

features.name: Custom Timer Builder Mode
features.description: Build custom sequences of exercise, rest, and repeat blocks, set names and durations, compile them into a runnable queue and control playback like Preset Timer mode.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Custom" tab -> Click Add Exercise button -> Type in the Name input for a given block -> Type in the Duration (seconds) for an exercise block -> Click Add Rest button -> Type in the Duration (seconds) for rest -> Click Add Repeat Group button -> Click "Start Workout" arrow to compile the blocks into a sequence and start execution -> Observe Circular Timer countdown and Workout Timeline -> Click "Start/Pause" pill button to pause and resume playback

Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Custom" tab -> Click Add Exercise button -> Type in the Name input for a given block -> Type a non-numeric or negative value into the Duration (seconds) field -> Click outside the field or attempt to start the workout -> Observe validation error or input rejection preventing invalid duration

Navigate to / -> Click Segmented Control "Custom" tab -> Click Add Repeat Group button -> Attempt to edit nested child blocks inside the repeat group (drag/drop or add children) -> Observe read-only placeholder UI indicating nested block editing is disabled in this MVP (see known limitations in src/modes/CustomBuilder.tsx)

routes.description: Main application page with segmented control for Stopwatch, Custom Timer, and Preset Timer modes

features.name: Stopwatch Mode
features.description: A simple stopwatch that counts up from zero
user_interactions: Click Segmented Control "Stopwatch" tab
Click "Start" button to begin timing
Click "Pause" button to pause timing
Click "Stop" button to reset timing

features.name: Preset Timer Mode
features.description: Users can select a pre-configured workout timer format (Tabata, EMOM, AMRAP, etc.) and run it
user_interactions: Click Segmented Control "Presets" tab
Scroll through carousel of predefined workout templates
Click a template card to select it
Click "Start [Template Name]" to begin the compiled workout queue
Click "Start/Pause" pill button to control playback
Click "Skip" arrow to move to the next step early
View Circular Timer countdown and Workout Timeline

features.name: Custom Timer Builder Mode
features.description: Users can build their own sequence of exercise, rest, and repeat blocks
user_interactions: Click Segmented Control "Custom" tab
Click Add Exercise button
Click Add Rest button
Click Add Repeat Group button
Type in the Name input for a given block
Type in the Duration (seconds) for an exercise or rest block
Click "X" to delete a block
Click "Start Workout" arrow to compile the blocks into a sequence and start execution
Control playback identically to Preset Timer Mode

known_limitations.issue: Nested block editing in CustomBuilder is disabled for this MVP phase
known_limitations.impact: Repeat blocks render a read-only UI placeholder instead of allowing drag-and-drop hierarchy.

known_limitations.issue: Audio cues use synthetic Web Audio beeps (ticking bomb and flatline)
known_limitations.impact: Requires user interaction with the document before sounds can magically play due to browser autoplay policies.
